methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Black Forest Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $3.25B

data.narrative:
NextSilicon, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Elad Raz, has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on two methodologies triangulated against a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private peers including Figure AI, Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, Skild AI, and Together AI, spanning Series A through Series C.

The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, using the $200M total raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $380M. The peer-median approach examines 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $14.0B and a range of $3.2B to $39.0B, reflecting meaningful dispersion within frontier AI valuations at this stage.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range with a low of $323M, a median of $7.2B, and a high of $16.1B. The wide spread—spanning nearly two orders of magnitude—reflects the acute uncertainty inherent in early-stage frontier tech valuations, where stage and sector momentum can override traditional growth metrics. The peer-median result dominates the upper bound, driven by recent market repricing of AI infrastructure and model development. Key uncertainties include the company's specific technical differentiation, revenue trajectory, and capital efficiency relative to the named peer set.
